posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee for The International Union of Elevator Constructors, Local Union #1
posting.unionName: International Union of Elevator Constructors, Local Union #1
posting.locationSummary: Long Island City / New York City area
posting.applicationSummary: Request an application online at www.neieprecruitment.org, then complete the emailed application and submit it by July 3, 2026 at 5:00 p.m.
posting.applicationHours: Online recruitment opens June 8, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. and ends June 19, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. Applications must be submitted by July 3, 2026 at 5:00 p.m.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Limited-application recruitment. Recruitment will stop when 2,500 applications have been submitted, whichever comes first.
posting.applicationFeeDetails: A $25 application fee is required. Payment accepted by postal money order, debit card, or credit card. No cash or personal checks. Fee waivers may be requested with verifiable proof of financial need.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from job sites and required classes at the approved school.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug screening after selection and before enrollment, at the sponsor's expense.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED.
Must pass the Elevator Industry Aptitude Test with a score of at least 70 out of 100.
Must present a government-issued photo ID at the time of the aptitude test.
Must pay a $25 application fee, unless a waiver is approved with verifiable proof of financial need.
Fee payment may be made by postal money order, debit card, or credit card; no cash or personal checks accepted.
Must pass a drug screening after selection and before enrollment, at the sponsor's expense.
Must pass a post-offer physical administered by a physician, at the sponsor's expense.
Must take a tool assessment test covering basic tools, their use, and basic measuring skills.
Must have reliable transportation to and from job sites and required classes at the approved school.

Must be physically able to perform the work of an Elevator-Escalator Constructor and Modernizer, including working around chemicals, lubricants, solvents, asbestos, and silica; using required PPE; performing repetitive stooping, crouching, bending, and heavy lifting up to 100 pounds; climbing and working on scaffolds, stairs, ladders, and in elevator machine rooms, overhead spaces, and confined spaces; standing or walking most of the time; reading small markings on electrical conductors and machines; working in noisy, dark, dusty, dirty, hot, and cold conditions; and communicating orally in loud work environments.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be 18 or older, have a high school diploma or equivalent, pass aptitude and tool tests, and be able to meet physical, transportation, and post-selection screening requirements.
posting.contactSummary: For further information, contact Elevator Constructors Local 1 at (718) 707-9450.
